title: Control Flow (if, switch, loops)
summary: Control flow decides which code runs and when.
if statements run code only when a condition is true.

sections.text: Code does not always run straight from top to bottom. Sometimes it should skip a part, choose between paths, or repeat the same step. Control flow is how you steer it.

sections.title: Making Decisions
sections.text: An if statement runs a block of code only when its condition is true. You can add an else for what to do otherwise. When you have many possible cases, a switch can be a cleaner way to pick the matching one.

sections.title: Repeating with Loops
sections.text: A loop repeats a block of code. A for loop runs a set number of times, like counting from one to ten. A while loop keeps going as long as a condition stays true. Loops save you from writing the same line over and over.

media.alt: An if branching two ways, beside a loop arrow repeating

sections.title: A Recipe with Choices and Repeats
sections.text: A recipe says if the dough is sticky, add flour. That is an if. It also says stir for twenty turns, that is a loop, repeating one action. Control flow turns a flat list of steps into a recipe that reacts and repeats.

media.alt: A recipe branching on a condition, then repeating a stir step

sections.title: Steering Your Code
items: Use an if to run code only when something is true.
Add an else for the other case.
Use a switch when choosing among many fixed options.
Use a for loop to repeat a set number of times.
Use a while loop to repeat until a condition changes.

sections.text: A loop with a condition that never becomes false will run forever and freeze the page. This is called an infinite loop, and it is one of the most common beginner mistakes.

terms.definition: Deciding which code runs and when.
Runs code only when a condition is true.
Runs code when the if condition is false.

questions.text: When does an if statement run its code?
options: Only when its condition is true
questions.explanation: An if statement runs its block only when the condition is true.
A loop repeats a block of code, saving you from rewriting it.

options: A loop whose condition never becomes false, so it runs forever
questions.explanation: An infinite loop never stops because its condition never turns false.
